blurb: A smooth blue-green ribbed barrel with widely spaced clusters of amber, slightly curved spines. Slow and sculptural — a proper little sun-baked keg. (Genus best-guess for now.)

care.light: Full, direct sun — barrels want as much as you can give and colour up and spine up best in it. Acclimate gradually before any big jump in light to avoid scorch.
care.water: Soak-and-dry and sparing: a deep drink in warm growth, then bone dry before the next. Keep it dry in winter.
care.soil: A very gritty, fast-draining cactus mix.
care.temperature: Room temperature and warm in summer; keep above ~35°F and dry when cold.
care.dormancy: A summer grower; rest it cool and dry through winter.
care.feeding: A dilute low-nitrogen cactus feed once or twice in summer.
care.potting: Slow-growing; repot infrequently and handle with folded newspaper or thick gloves around the spines.
care.propagation: Usually grown from seed; it may eventually offset at the base.
care.bloom: Cup-shaped flowers near the crown on mature plants.
care.troubles: Rot from overwatering or cold-and-wet; sunburn if moved into strong sun too fast; mealybugs.
care.toxicity: Non-toxic — but the stout spines mean careful handling.
